findings.title: No first-party scripts while pipelines still need deterministic checks
findings.detail: Irene is explicitly barred from calling APIs or executing scripts, which keeps the persona clean but pushes all validation (envelope shape, staging paths, ID invariants) onto humans or sibling tooling. Automators and CI get no repo-local helper to fail fast before burning tokens on a bad run.
findings.action: Add optional companion scripts (e.g. validate context envelope YAML/JSON, scaffold staging filenames from LP/NS/SB IDs) documented from SKILL or Marcus workflow—not executed by Irene, but callable by pipelines and the orchestrator.

findings.title: Headless contract is prose-only—no machine schema for automators
findings.detail: Inbound/outbound fields for Marcus are listed in bullet form but there is no JSON Schema or OpenAPI-style artifact for validators, test fixtures, or codegen. Headless success depends on consistent field names across orchestrator implementations.
findings.action: Publish a versioned JSON Schema (or equivalent) for context envelope and structured return under references/ or _bmad/, and reference it from SKILL.md so headless and CI consumers share one contract.

findings.title: Experts with a complete brief still get the same interactive ramp as novices
findings.detail: Interactive mode greets with course state but offers no 'I already have objectives, constraints, and artifact type—skip discovery' path. Power users who live in the templates may find the agent slower than delegating manually.
findings.action: Document a 'spec-first' or YOLO interactive mode: user pastes or points to a filled mini-brief (content_type, module_lesson, LOs, constraints); Irene validates and jumps straight to IA→delegation→assembly.

findings.title: Interactive activation loads context before clarifying user intent
findings.detail: On Activation batches parallel reads of config, memory, and course_context with no mandatory 'why are you here?' beat first. For exploratory or ad-hoc requests, scanning full course state before intent can add noise and steer the conversation prematurely.
findings.action: Reorder interactive flow: one-line intent capture (create vs revise vs analyze source) then load only the files needed for that intent; keep full parallel batch for headless where the envelope already states intent.

findings.title: Editorial sub-skills unavailable or skipped in constrained environments
findings.detail: Every piece expects bmad-editorial-review-prose and structure review on assemblies. In a minimal IDE, missing skills, denied tools, or budget limits could block the documented path with no documented fallback sequence.
findings.action: Add explicit degradation: e.g. PQ-only review with a checklist, or 'degraded_run: true' in outbound to Marcus when editorial steps are skipped, plus criteria for when that is acceptable.

findings.title: Long sessions risk dropped pairing state when context compacts
findings.detail: Multi-artifact workflows depend on consistent LP/NS/SB/AB IDs and pairing references. Compaction or thread switches could lose the invariant that templates assume without a single serialized 'pairing manifest' artifact.
findings.action: Emit or require a lightweight pairing manifest file in staging (YAML/JSON) updated after each assembly step; reload it at session start alongside index.md.

findings.title: No explicit pause, handoff, or resume playbook for interactive users
findings.detail: Save Memory checkpoints index and patterns, but a user who must stop mid-delegation gets little guidance on what to persist, what Marcus should see, or how to resume without re-explaining.
findings.action: Add a short 'session interrupt' subsection: required fields to write to index.md, suggested message back to Marcus, and resume checklist (reload envelope + sidecar + last artifact path).

findings.title: Capability transitions lack soft-gate elicitation
findings.detail: The capability table is linear (IA→LO→…→SM) without 'anything else before we delegate?' moments. Users often volunteer constraints or source material at transition points; the skill does not cue capture there.
findings.action: At natural boundaries (after instructional analysis, before writer selection), add optional soft gates: confirm scope, invite extra constraints, then proceed—aligns with capture-don't-interrupt for out-of-band detail.

findings.title: Primary artifacts are human-facing only—downstream LLMs get full prose
findings.detail: Six artifact types are rich for humans and tool specialists; chained LLM workflows (another agent summarizing for a dashboard) would pay a large token tax with no parallel distillate.
findings.action: Optionally append a compact 'LLM distillate' block per artifact (objectives, Bloom tags, pairing IDs, downstream params only) for orchestration and logging—not replacing the main template.

findings.title: Single pedagogical review lens before finalize
findings.detail: PQ capability plus editorial prose/structure covers quality but not explicit multi-lens review (skeptic vs opportunity vs accessibility). High-stakes med-ed content could benefit from fan-out before sign-off.
findings.action: For flagship assets, document optional parallel review: e.g. invoke adversarial or edge-case reviewer on assessment briefs and dialogue scripts when Marcus sets review_depth: high.

findings.title: Only two coarse modes named—no explicit Guided vs Autonomous spectrum
findings.detail: Headless vs interactive maps to orchestration vs human, but not to user skill level within interactive (hand-holding vs minimal prompts).
findings.action: Add optional run_mode semantics: guided (step confirmations), standard, autonomous interactive (confirm only on escalation)—mirroring three-mode architecture where it helps without bloating default SKILL.

findings.title: Environment check lists paths that may be missing on first clone
findings.detail: Init verifies course_context, style-bible, staging—if any are absent, the user sees 'verify exists' without a scripted scaffold or fallback tree beyond prose.
findings.action: Pair init with a one-time directory/file scaffold list or Marcus-owned setup script so automators and first-timers do not dead-end on empty folders.

findings.title: Optional envelope fields can contradict without resolution rules
findings.detail: user_constraints, style_bible_sections, and source_materials can conflict (e.g. tone vs style bible section). No priority order or conflict detection is documented.
findings.action: Define precedence: e.g. explicit user_constraints override style_bible_sections; log conflicts in writer_delegation_log for Marcus.

findings.title: Out-of-scope instructional detail during discovery has no capture-and-defer hook
findings.detail: Principles stress tracing content to objectives; users in flow may dump clinical nuance before objectives are firm. Redirecting to 'not yet' can kill useful detail.
findings.action: Add a 'parking lot' convention in sidecar or staging (deferred insights list) merged after LO lock—implements capture-don't-interrupt without weakening objective traceability.

findings.title: Multi-capability workflows lack visible progress for the user
findings.detail: Capabilities span analysis through save-memory; direct users do not see a phase checklist or ETA when multiple writers and revision rounds are involved.
findings.action: Surface a simple phase tracker in replies: current phase, completed artifacts, pending delegations—optional one-liner updated each turn.

findings.title: Clear split between headless delegation and interactive direct use
findings.detail: Context envelope requirements, return shape, and parallel config load are well specified for Marcus-driven runs; degradation paths for missing LOs and scope are documented.

skill_understanding.purpose: Design pedagogically grounded medical education artifacts (six types), delegate prose to specialist writers, review for alignment, assemble templates with downstream routing—usually under Marcus orchestration.
skill_understanding.primary_user: Course authors and production orchestrators (Marcus) delegating content work; secondary: instructors invoking Irene directly for planning.
key_assumptions: course_context.yaml and style bible are available or explicitly substituted
BMad writer and editorial agents are invocable when following full protocol
Staging and sidecar paths exist or are created via init
Learning objectives are authoritative from context—not invented

user_journeys.summary: Discovers Irene via trigger phrases; loads many files on activation; may be overwhelmed by capability matrix and writer names without a short guided tour.
friction_points: Jargon-heavy capability table without a minimal 'start here' path
Unclear which external agents must exist for a 'full' run
bright_spots: Friendly interactive greeting with course state
init.md and memory-system explain sidecar setup

user_journeys.summary: Has objectives and format already; wants immediate delegation briefs and minimal ceremony.
friction_points: No documented fast lane past discovery in interactive mode
Revision round caps may feel slow without upfront writer tuning from memory
bright_spots: Writer selection matrix and required brief fields reward precision

user_journeys.summary: Invoked Irene for slides or QA by mistake.
friction_points: Redirect message is good but does not auto-route to the right agent
bright_spots: Explicit Does Not Do and redirect string in SKILL.md

user_journeys.summary: Valid but odd input—partial envelope, conflicting constraints, or lesson split mid-run.
friction_points: Scope split requests rely on Marcus relay
bright_spots: Degradation section covers missing LOs and cognitive overload flagging

user_journeys.summary: Missing files, read-only disk, or editorial skills unavailable.
friction_points: init verifies paths but does not create them
No fallback when editorial review cannot run
bright_spots: Ad-hoc mode read-only rules are clear

user_journeys.summary: CI or another agent supplies headless envelope expecting structured return and file paths.
friction_points: No repo scripts for validation without LLM
bright_spots: Required and optional envelope fields are listed
Outbound fields include artifact_paths and downstream_routing for chaining

autonomous_assessment.suggested_output_contract: JSON status object with status, artifact_paths[], artifact_type, downstream_routing, writer_delegation_log, pairing_references, recommendations; plus written files under course-content/staging/ with stable ID prefixes.
required_inputs: Optional but often needed: user_constraints, source_materials, existing_content_refs for coherence
autonomous_assessment.notes: Headless delegation path is well specified relative to many skills; full autonomy still depends on invoking writer and editorial agents. Adding schema + optional CLI validation would move operational reliability toward headless-ready for pipelines.

top_insights.title: Treat orchestration reliability as a first-class product
top_insights.detail: The creative unlock is not more pedagogy copy—it is contracts, manifests, and optional validators so Marcus, CI, and humans do not diverge on envelope shape or pairing IDs.
top_insights.action: Ship JSON Schema for I/O, a pairing manifest file, and documented degraded paths when editorial agents are absent.

top_insights.title: Interactive mode should borrow facilitator patterns already implicit in production
top_insights.detail: Intent-before-ingestion, soft gates, and parking lots reduce friction without changing Irene's core pedagogy-first identity.
top_insights.action: Add three short procedural blocks to SKILL: interactive first beat, transition soft gates, resume-after-interrupt.

summary.assessment: Irene is unusually strong on delegation structure, headless vocabulary, and degradation for instructional risks—the main creative gap is tooling around that design: machine-readable contracts, resume/interrupt UX, facilitator beats in interactive mode, and explicit fallbacks when editorial agents or paths are missing. The boldest practical upgrade is a versioned envelope/return schema plus a pairing manifest so long runs and automators stay coherent.
